key_insight: A high-selectivity oxygen ICP-RIE process with a SiO2 hard mask enables complete through-etching of thick single-crystal diamond membranes, followed by room-temperature bonding to silica for scalable diamond-on-silica heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a contamination-free fabrication route for integrated quantum photonics and sensing devices that need deep-patterned diamond membranes on transparent substrates.
method: Single-layer SiO2 hard mask for diamond etching
Multi-step oxygen-based ICP-RIE with ~15:1 diamond-to-SiO2 selectivity
O2 plasma activation plus sodium silicate interlayer for room-temperature bonding
evidence_signal: The abstract reports full through-etching of a 16 μm-thick NV-doped diamond membrane with well-defined sidewalls, preserved surface roughness, negligible micromasking, and no detectable parasitic photoluminescence after bonding.
watch_item: The room-temperature bond relies on an interlayer and surface activation, so bond uniformity and long-term mechanical/thermal stability may need careful process control.
paper_title: Scalable Fabrication of Diamond-on-Silica Heterostructures via High-Selectivity Deep ICP-RIE and Room-Temperature Bonding
